steps: "At least 40" means x $\geq$ 40; "no more than 65" means x $\leq$ 65.
Both constraints must hold simultaneously, so combine them into one compound inequality.
The compound inequality is $40 \leq x \leq 65$, which means x is between 40 and 65 inclusive.
That matches choice C — C is correct.

choices.elimination:
• This captures 'at least 40' but places no upper bound on speed.
  • The car could travel at 200 mph under this inequality, which violates the 'no more than 65' condition.
  • Eliminated because the upper bound is missing.

• This would mean the car travels at 65 mph or more, which misses the lower bound of 40.
  • It also excludes speeds between 40 and 64, which are permitted by the problem.
  • Eliminated because it misrepresents both constraints.

• 'At least 40' gives $x \geq 40$; 'no more than 65' gives $x \leq 65$.
  • Writing both together yields $40 \leq x \leq 65$, which is exactly this choice.
  • That matches choice C — C is correct.

• 105 = 40 + 65, but summing the two speed limits is not a valid mathematical operation for expressing this constraint.
  • This inequality allows speeds as low as 0 and as high as 105, neither bound correctly reflecting the problem.
  • Eliminated because it doesn't represent either the lower or upper bound correctly.
